name: The White House
subject: Recovery, Relief, and Reaching New Heights
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0aba3752279f618f50b677d87e4f4deeee87a1108634a513b1d4fbe7e2b9f2e0a173c99f3ba02b10ce6d0008fe26a571b7b68d74c6937885

President Trump's Weekly Address

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=e59cdc06608ff96d8f008a2f1bf7d7c0162d6f4a4d29d6b4abf34837d3dc7eeee87ea73230179a97eb2a1f6ffd535dacef3a1b8f3e12aaa1

Military Leaders Visit The White House
Last night, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump welcomed senior military officials and their spouses to a dinner at the White House, during which they discussed the many challenges faced by military families.

Prior to dinner, the President participated in a briefing with the military leaders to review progress in protecting the homeland, advancing American prosperity, pursuing peace through strength, and promoting positive American influence abroad.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f0207ea9397bf1d9ea536570735231238cae7168d142fabfb72b9a7853fd31e89191225f5de3a6921fa28610ffe63d7a88e597db7595aeed
Read More.

“America Will Return to the Moon – And Go Beyond”
On Wednesday, Vice President Mike Pence published an op-ed in The Wall Street Journal preceding the first meeting of the National Space Council in nearly 25 years. President Trump has revived the Space Council to assist his Administration in articulating and implementing long-term goals for the American space program. Two of those goals include re-establishing American presence on the moon and becoming the first Nation to bring mankind to Mars.
At the Thursday morning meeting, Vice President Pence hearkened back to the early days of the American space program as the inspiration for renewing America’s competitive status in space exploration.

“The sight of Sputnik blinking across that October sky spurred America to action. We refused to accept a future in space written by the enemies of freedom, and so the United States of America vowed to claim our rightful place as the undisputed leader in the exploration of the heavens,” the Vice President said. “And twelve years later, with ‘one giant leap for mankind,’ America led in space.... We gather here today to renew this same mission in our time.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bfc10b0f264e2f429385bbe6f552263a38d1d23188ea4a7a4e0dbf90aa700b0dccd593bddc8ad6f9bf815fddc5677e455b5728709cfd6dd2
See more from the first Space Meeting.

Hurricane Relief in Florida, Puerto Rico, and the U.S. Virgin Islands

Vice President Mike Pence traveled to Florida on Thursday to receive a briefing on FEMA relief efforts following Hurricanes Irma and Maria and meet with individuals who have been impacted by the storms. FEMA also provided updates on Hurricane Maria in its daily briefing on Thursday morning. Key points included:

- We continue to make progress through the unified efforts of the federal government, working hand-in-hand with Puerto Rico, its municipalities, businesses and voluntary agencies on the island.
- Our goal is to help Puerto Ricans return to their routines, which will progress at a faster rate as more businesses open and communities are able to support their residents.
- Although it will be some time before communities truly recover, residents are beginning to see some services being provided in their communities.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=3e010969f73cd74db97c7d48924b4719020f52cbc536d1142169ea28bdae2cb78cf258b8a2f1d62ae5257b46c276c5f0c7eb70428215a709
See more from the Vice President’s trip to Florida.

Photo of the Day

President Trump meets with senior U.S. military leaders | October 5, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump hosted an event at the White House honoring Hispanic Heritage Month. Later, the President signed a Proclamation declaring October 6th National Manufacturing Day.

Vice President Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence are in Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands to survey hurricane recovery efforts and meet with individuals affected by the storms.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a5ffe440b1c0ffcaea5d7c5d4114130dbe18d4d54001e92320918d3dcd777f5750c4cecdf2012ddd13f9e88174910c59ef737b084654bb6d
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=13fecebad4bfab59e8ca18855650c2d3b490a60972719eb66d5801ebe73154f9f5bedefda3fdc57f3c0c9aa54c34bdb45e41f4ed9ce4e775
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d447c6aa2fcf585296c692fd6a1821cbffc618a5a9d1ff6f602683bbf98a0c8ab2dbe988f0e30ecbb020b35a5e7a3fe907
Unsubscribe

name: Team Kaine
subject: BREAKING: Trump attacks women's rights (again)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Hi -- this is Jess, Tim's digital director. Late last night, some really bad news began trending on Twitter. The Trump Administration was expected to announce it was rolling back the guarantee that insurance plans cover contraception and birth control under the Affordable Care Act.

**[Today, that expectation became a reality.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=0qBFfLDjVkVJu%2FWfVuK%2BMoxYj3MCIiWH)**

As of now, all employers will no longer have to cover no-copay contraception as a part of their health insurance plans. That means bosses could decide whether the women who work for them can get birth control. Access to affordable contraceptive care could be threatened for as many as *62 million* women across the country.

**[Plain and simple, this is an attack on women -- and a terrifying reversal of our hard-fought progress.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=k3jxyxrxPaQkONSHJKub50CISzmLG7uY)**

**The ability to decide whether and when to have children is part and parcel of women's rights. Taking away that ability, through limiting access to affordable birth control, is a blatant attempt from the Trump Administration to control women's health care and ultimately undermine a woman's basic right to make her own decisions about her life.**

We've seen this attitude towards women over and over and over again from Trump and his allies. From revoking President Obama's Fair Pay and Safe Workplaces Order to rolling back Title IX assurances for sexual assault survivors on college campuses -- this Administration is hurting women.

**[I've had enough. Add your name to tell President Trump that you've had enough, too.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=fbQ24z2%2BlDGwY1pajH672oxYj3MCIiWH)**

Thanks.

-jess

Jessalyn Reid, Team Kaine Digital Director

subject: This tax plan will actually change your life
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc
body:
Friend,

Next April, when you're filling out your taxes, you will notice some huge changes. And trust me, you're going to like these changes.

I was talking with Paul Ryan this week about his plan for tax reform -- about how your taxes will be simpler, fairer, and lower.

His plan **radically simplifies** the tax code, reducing the size of the form to a post card!

It also **removes loopholes making the system fairer, and it will lower tax rates** for every income level.

And **this plan is built for growth**. It will inject trillions of dollars into the American economy -- making everyday life easier for all Americans.

Bottom line? **Y****ou will notice the difference when you file your taxes next April.**

There's no doubt about it, our country needs this. [**If you agree it 's time for conservative tax reform, stand with me today >> **](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MzA5MTI5NzgS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE0MDczNDg2NAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

subject: NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Representative Shea-Porter's retirement
body:
**NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester statement on Representative Shea-Porter's retirement**
**For Immediate Release:** October 6, 2017**Contact:** Matt Sparks, (603) 225-9341
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding the announced retirement of Representative Shea-Porter: “The news today from Representative Shea-Porter, following her record of obstructing all meaningful reform in Washington since the election, is welcome news for Granite staters who have grown tired of her ineffective leadership. New Hampshire has a fantastic opportunity to replace her with a Republican representative who will work for the people of New Hampshire’s First Congressional District.”
###

subject: Add your name - tell Republicans to leave women's rights alone:
body:
Stand with Democrats against the GOP's attacks on women.
---
**Trump and the GOP are attacking women's reproductive rights from all sides. Add your name -- tell Republicans to stop attacking women's rights:

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-the-Republican-attack-on-women1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20171006_xh_nd&source=em_20171006_xh_nd")**
This week looks more like 1917 than 2017 for women across the country. First, House Republicans passed a bill that would ban all abortions after 20 weeks. And today, the Trump administration announced that they're rolling back a mandate requiring employer-provided health insurance to cover contraception -- a rule that has helped at least 55 million women across the country access care.

Let's be clear: This is a direct assault on women's rights by Republicans, Derek.

>From the moment they took office, Republicans have done everything they can to undermine women's rights -- from imposing the global gag rule, which blocks any foreign aid to organizations that even mention abortion, to trying to defund Planned Parenthood every chance they get.

Instead of writing off more than half of our country, maybe Republicans could actually do something to help women. Just a thought.

**Add your name now -- tell Republicans to STOP their assault on women's health and rights:

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-the-Republican-attack-on-women1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20171006_xh_nd&source=em_20171006_xh_nd")**
Thanks,

Xochitl

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: the time is NOW
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __ The violence and bloodshed in Las Vegas last week has taken innocent lives, shattered families and left us all in pain. While we have united to grieve for those who were lost in this tragedy, we should also unite to reduce the terrible toll of gun violence in our country. Prayers alone are not enough. Those who try to stifle debate by refusing to discuss gun safety measures in the aftermath of these massacres only guarantee that the carnage will continue.

In recent days, some Republicans have suggested they might support a ban on "bump stocks," a firearm accessory that transforms semiautomatic weapons into fully automatic killing machines. While that would be a step forward, it does not go nearly far enough to address the issues that make our country the leader in gun violence.

_We must take real action to reduce the daily toll of gun violence. We should require universal criminal background checks to close the gun show loophole. We should repeal the outrageous law that made the gun industry immune from lawsuits for its negligence. There are many other common sense measures we can take. I am begging my Republican colleagues to put politics aside and join us in taking action to address this life-or-death issue._

[**Sign here to tell Senate Republicans to support common sense gun legislation that will place the safety of American citizens above partisan politics >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd8/70cdc2ba/46a17a8f/397d99c/2160664225/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stop-gun-violence?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Sign+here+to+tell+Senate+Republicans+to+&utm_campaign=em100617&source=em100617")

My colleagues on the other side of the aisle have chosen time and time again to side with the NRA over the safety of Americans. After the tragic events this week, I urge them to reconsider and take steps that have been proven to save lives.

[**Join me in sending an unequivocal message that we must have action, and tell Senate Republicans now is the time for common sense gun reform >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bd8/70cdc2ba/46a17a8f/397d99d/2160664225/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stop-gun-violence?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Join+me+in+sending+an+unequivocal+messag&utm_campaign=em100617&source=em100617")

Let's come together and save lives.

Chris Van Hollen

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: this fight is too important
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Last weekend, Republicans allowed federal funding for the Children's Health Insurance Program to expire. |
---

| | Peter,

Last weekend, Republicans allowed federal funding for the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP) -- a program that protects the children of some of Indiana's most vulnerable families -- to expire. **Because they failed to act, nearly 100,000 kids in Indiana could lose their health care by April.**

Do you know what makes this worse, Peter? **This was totally preventable.** Republicans were distracted by their failed attempts to repeal the Affordable Care Act and allowed CHIP's funding to expire. Now the health care of our children is in jeopardy. **I 'm going to do everything I can to restore funding, but I need your help.**

**[Will you add your name to mine to call on my colleagues to get to work and fund CHIP?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c240c/7377f838/34895272/51fb9014/3239657539/VEsH/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/demand-republicans--fund-chip")**

House Republicans were too focused on scoring political points instead of working together to protect and provide the care children need. The time for partisan games is over -- health care for our kids is too important to be yanked around like some bargaining chip.

**[Add your name: Stand with me and demand my colleagues fund CHIP and defend our children's health care.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c240c/7377f838/34895272/51fb9014/3239657539/VEsE/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/demand-republicans--fund-chip")**

Thank you,

Joe

subject: [Add your name] What Trump JUST did to women's rights
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c626d/24c69fd9/8bdc88ba/189d0fc1/3379319538/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEJ13N_digital_email_20171006_TrumpContraceptionRR_inact&source=WQENEJ13N_digital_email_20171006_TrumpContraceptionRR_inact")
---

Peter,

President Trump just issued a new attack on women's rights: He's rolling back Obamacare's birth control coverage requirement, which let millions of American women access contraception free of charge.

Simply put, he's throwing open the floodgates for groups to discriminate against women. We're having **none of it.**

**[Add your name to stand up to Trump's repeated attacks on women and hold him accountable.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c626d/24c69fd9/8bdc88ba/189d0fc2/3379319538/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20171006_email_TrumpContraception?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+to+stand+up+to+Trumps+repe&utm_campaign=WQENEJ13N_digital_email_20171006_TrumpContraceptionRR_inact&source=WQENEJ13N_digital_email_20171006_TrumpContraceptionRR_inact")**

Affordable contraception is a no-brainer, and the proof is in the numbers.

Since the rule was originally passed as part of Obamacare, more than 55 million women gained contraceptive coverage without having to pay out-of-pocket. In 2013 alone, the rule collectively saved women $1.4 BILLION on birth control.

Contraception is essential to making sure that women are in control of their own lives -- that they can decide if, when, and with whom to start or grow a family.

An attack on a woman's right to affordable birth control is an attack on her right to be an equal citizen, plain and simple.

Stand up against Trump and commit to fighting relentlessly for women's rights. Add your name:

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Stop-Trump-Contraception-Attack](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c626d/24c69fd9/8bdc88ba/189d0fc3/3379319538/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20171006_email_TrumpContraception?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgStopTrumpContrac&utm_campaign=WQENEJ13N_digital_email_20171006_TrumpContraceptionRR_inact&source=WQENEJ13N_digital_email_20171006_TrumpContraceptionRR_inact")**

Thank you,

Jenna Kruse
Vice President of Research, EMILY's List

clean_body: We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | "" Peter, President Trump just issued a new attack on women's rights: He's rolling back Obamacare's birth control coverage requirement, which let millions of American women access contraception free of charge. Simply put, he's throwing open the floodgates for groups to discriminate against women. We're having none of it. "" Affordable contraception is a no-brainer, and the proof is in the numbers. Since the rule was originally passed as part of Obamacare, more than 55 million women gained contraceptive coverage without having to pay out-of-pocket. In 2013 alone, the rule collectively saved women $1.4 BILLION on birth control. Contraception is essential to making sure that women are in control of their own lives -- that they can decide if, when, and with whom to start or grow a family. An attack on a woman's right to affordable birth control is an attack on her right to be an equal citizen, plain and simple. Stand up against Trump and commit to fighting relentlessly for women's rights. body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| **The 1st Annual Team Holcomb Fall Dinner - Friday, November 17**
* * *
We've got big news to share this week! Instead of our usual Indiana Republican Party Fall Dinner this year, we've teamed up with Eric Holcomb for Indiana to host the [1st Annual Team Holcomb Fall Dinner](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=93e4448397&e=1acd9bb3d6) on Friday, November 17, 2017, in downtown Indianapolis...and [you're invited](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=83256b800a&e=1acd9bb3d6)! Thanks to our one-of-a-kind joint fundraising committee, every contribution made at this dinner will go directly to helping Governor Holcomb's campaign and the Indiana Republican Party. This will help ensure success for our candidates up and down the ballot in 2018.

And, as you already know we like to do things a little differently around here, so this 1st Annual Team Holcomb Fall Dinner has a very special theme: Celebrating Our Military. Special guests, and Hoosiers heroes, **Josh Bleill** and **Sammy L. Davis** will join Governor **Eric Holcomb** and me in honoring those who have served and continue to serve us at home and around the globe. Bleill, an Iraq War Veteran and former Colts Community Spokesperson, and Davis, a Medal of Honor Recipient who is frequently called "The Real Forrest Gump," will both speak during our dinner program. In addition, each of our VIP sponsorship packages include additional seating specially reserved for veterans, members of our military and their families. It's our way of saying THANK YOU!

Please [join us for this special event](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ab70dd0a68&e=1acd9bb3d6) to benefit Team Holcomb and celebrate the brave men and women who protect our nation. For more information on our special guests or to reserve your spot at the dinner, click [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=14fb37c482&e=1acd9bb3d6).

\- Kyle Hupfer

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer and Governor Holcomb greet each other on stage at the 2017 Indiana Republican Party Spring Dinner.)
---
| |
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
---
| This week, [Governor Holcomb** ** published an op-ed](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7e90381d4b&e=1acd9bb3d6) in the _Indianapolis Star_ explaining why, from the moment he was sworn in as governor, he has made combating the opioid epidemic one of his top priorities. In addition, the Governor also this week unveiled the [Next Level Recovery website](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d169951797&e=1acd9bb3d6) that will act as a platform for Hoosiers to get the help they need in fighting addiction. The website aims to provide Hoosiers with easily accessible resources for prevention, recovery and treatment. You can visit the Next Level Recovery website [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=aa6e979b71&e=1acd9bb3d6).

And, of course, there were more economic development and infrastructure announcements this week, too! On Tuesday, Governor Holcomb joined local leaders in Fishers to announce that music marketing start-up Fuzic will be [expanding and creating](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a8360684e5&e=1acd9bb3d6) over 250 jobs and building a $3.7 million headquarters. Then on Thursday, the Governor went a little further north to Kokomo where he [awarded the city a Community Crossings grant](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=87db578cb1&e=1acd9bb3d6). This grant helps cities and towns upgrade their infrastructure and workforce development efforts simultaneously.
---
|
---
|
---
**_New Study Says Democrat Donnelly is Least Effective Democrat in Senate_** We've been saying this for a while, and now a new survey backs it up. The least effective Democrat in the United States Senate has been revealed and guess who took the crown? [Democrat Joe Donnelly](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=acb344f406&e=1acd9bb3d6). Based on a recent survey conducted by the Center for Effective Lawmaking, Democrat Donnelly is at the bottom - the very bottom - of the list of most effective members of the Senate. The nonpartisan center based its rankings on various aspects, including how far a senators' bills get in the legislative process, how many become law, etc. Of course, this news comes as little surprise to those who have paid attention to Democrat Donnelly's time in the Senate. But now it's officially been exposed for all the world to see and to know.
|

subject: the team's note on gun violence prevention
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Florida Democratic Party
body:
Did you see the team's note, friend?

[**If you want to see action on gun safety legislation, we need you with us. Click here to add your name so Congress hears your voice >>**](http://act.floridadems. "https://secure.floridadems.org/page/signup/fdp-demand-commonsense-gun-control?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&occupation=&employer=&source=em100617")

-- Johanna

**Begin original message sent October 5, 2017:**
> Friend, the time for inaction is over.
>
> Republicans have blocked legislation to prevent gun violence for years. Now, Americans are demanding common sense solutions from leaders.
>
> [**Please, if you think it 's time to pass common sense gun safety legislation, can you click here and add your name?**](http://act.floridadems. "https://secure.floridadems.org/page/signup/fdp-demand-commonsense-gun-control?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&occupation=&employer=&source=em100617")
>
> We stood together to defend Obamacare, and we showed the power of our collective action.
>
> Now, we must bring that energy to passing common sense gun reforms immediately.
>
> [**If you agree, we need you to stand with us. Click here to stand in support of gun safety reforms >>**](http://act.floridadems. "https://secure.floridadems.org/page/signup/fdp-demand-commonsense-gun-control?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&occupation=&employer=&source=em100617")
>
> Thank you,
> Florida Democrats

---

| Paid for by Florida Democratic Party. Not Authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
---
